column3: ATX is the full-size desktop form factor listed for this objective.
column4:
Distinction

Use ATX when expansion room and standard desktop case fit matter more than minimizing board size.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.34; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze Motherboard FormFactor Source::Messer-v170

column3: PCIe is the modern expansion interface used by many adapter cards.
column4:
Distinction

PCIe replaced the older PCI bus for most modern expansion-card scenarios.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.35; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze PCIe ExpansionCards Source::Messer-v170

column3: SATA is commonly shortened as Serial ATA.
column4:
Distinction

In Objective 3.5, SATA is a motherboard connector type; in Objective 3.4, it also appears as a storage-device interface.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze SATA Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: Objective 3.5 lists both SATA and eSATA as motherboard connector types.
column4:
Distinction

The leading "e" is the reminder that eSATA is for external SATA connections.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze SATA Storage Source::Messer-v170

column3: UEFI is the modern firmware interface listed in Objective 3.5.
column4:
Distinction

UEFI is firmware, not an operating system. It configures platform behavior before the OS takes over.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.36; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze UEFI BIOS Source::Messer-v170

column3: A TPM provides hardware-backed cryptographic functions for a local system.
column4:
Distinction

A TPM is tied to one device, which is why it is commonly associated with local device encryption and trust.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 pp.37-38; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze HighYield TPM Encryption Source::Messer-v170

column3: An HSM is often used to protect cryptographic keys for many systems or services.
column4:
Distinction

An HSM is the centralized or shared-key counterpart to the single-system TPM concept.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.38; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze HSM Encryption Source::Messer-v170

column3: The official objective expands RISC as Reduced Instruction Set Computer.
column4:
Distinction

ARM is associated in this objective with efficient, low-power, low-heat systems.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.38; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze HighYield ARM CPU Source::Messer-v170

column2: x64 refers to a {{c1::64-bit}} PC CPU architecture.
column4:
Distinction

x64 is the 64-bit PC architecture term commonly contrasted with x86 in operating system and driver compatibility discussions.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.38; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze CPU Architecture Source::Messer-v170

column3: POST is part of the firmware startup process before the operating system loads.
column4:
Distinction

POST happens early in startup and checks core hardware before normal OS loading.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.36; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze BIOS POST Source::Messer-v170

column3: A NIC adds or replaces network connectivity.
column4:
Distinction

A NIC may be onboard or installed as an expansion card; Objective 3.5 lists it as an add-on card type.
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.38; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.5
column9: A+::220-1201::3.5 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::MotherboardsCPUsandAddonCards Cloze NIC ExpansionCards Source::Messer-v170
